title: SACRAMENTO – Assemblywoman Pilar Schiavo announced today that she successfully advanced six priority bills through Assembly Appropriations Committee. These bills move to the Assemb

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 464 eliminates costs for driver's licenses required for low-income individuals and people experiencing homelessness.",
    "AB 464 eliminates fees for access to vital records for individuals receiving public assistance.",
    "AB 519 establishes an Affordable Housing Finance Workgroup to streamline the application process for affordable housing developers.",
    "AB 519 aims to reduce the time and costs associated with affordable housing development according to a 2020 State Auditor report.",
    "AB 608 provides postpartum care from Comprehensive Perinatal Health Workers for new mothers and babies enrolled in Medi-Cal for a full year after pregnancy.",
    "AB 1133 centralizes information on proper concealed carry practices and establishes testing standards for concealed carry permits.",
    "AB 1359 provides additional sick days for healthcare workers to protect against unnecessary exposure to illness.",
    "AB 1359 details a minimum sick day accrual rate for health workers.",
    "AB 1423 prohibits the purchase or installation of Astroturf containing perfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) by schools and governments starting in 2024.",
    "AB 1423 prohibits the intentional use of PFAS in any Astroturf product manufactured or sold in California starting in 2025."
  ]
}
```
